other_observers: Brandon Percival who identified it originally Glenn Walbek Dawn Moon Joyce T. John Drummond Charles Thorton-Kolbe Scott and 2 teenage boy Schaums; Glenn and I saw the bird first but we failed to stop to I.D. it as we were trying to refind the Canada Warbler. Brandon identified it first.
specific_location: Ranch house next to HQ at The Chico Basin Ranch.
description: This was a perched female, and possibly the same one from Chico Basin Ranch as seen on 25 May 2008.  Perched, I only look at the wing and saw three brownish spots on the folded wing, the key for separating this nighthawk from Common and Antillean.  I did see it fly briefly and it was buffy underneath, but that is really all that I noticed.Behaviors: Mostly perced on horizontal limb.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
